notes: Yellow and purple floral morphs coexist within natural populations; study tests frequency-dependent reproductive success.
Light-pink, dark-pink and white floral morphs are documented within populations used for biochemical and transcriptomic comparison.
Multiple floral colour phenotypes segregate in natural populations and are analysed as an emerging polymorphism.
Species contains strongly differentiated populations, including predominantly single-colour populations and populations with both blue and white morphs at intermediate frequencies.
